lockingPolicy.lockReason: Regression scenarios encode the SST NO-DEFAULTS contract for 2 new HTTP-client timeout config keys; any future change to the scenario set requires explicit invalidation evidence per Gate G058/G059.

tdd.rationale: This IS a code change (2 Config field additions + 2 call-site migrations + 2 yaml additions + 6 new unit tests); NOT artifact-only. Red→Green evidence is mandatory.
tdd.redGreenEvidence: Red: 6 new unit tests authored against main MUST FAIL (the 2 Config fields do not exist; literals remain at the call-sites). Green: after migration to fail-loud Config-driven timeouts wired through both call-sites, the 6 tests pass.

scenarios.title: Missing single HTTP timeout env key produces a Validate() error naming the key
scenarios.liveTestExpectation: Table-driven over the 2 new keys; for each key, os.Unsetenv that key (other valid), call Config.Load()/Validate(), assert returned error contains the missing key name.

scenarios.title: Both HTTP timeout env keys missing produce a single consolidated error
scenarios.liveTestExpectation: Unset both simultaneously, call Load()/Validate(), assert the SINGLE returned error contains BOTH key names.

scenarios.title: Unparseable HTTP timeout value produces a Validate() error naming key and value
scenarios.liveTestExpectation: Table-driven over the 2 new keys; set each to "abc", call Load()/Validate(), assert error names both key and offending value.

scenarios.title: Zero or negative HTTP timeout is rejected with a range error
scenarios.liveTestExpectation: Table-driven over the 2 new keys; set each to "0" then "-1", call Validate(), assert range error naming the key.

scenarios.title: Financial-markets connector applies the config-derived timeout (adversarial non-default value)
scenarios.liveTestExpectation: Construct the connector with cfg.FinancialMarketsHTTPTimeoutSeconds = 7 (NOT the default 10). Assert connector.httpClient.Timeout == 7 * time.Second. Using a non-default value proves the value flows from config rather than being a coincidence with the pre-fix literal.

scenarios.title: OAuth tokenRequest applies the config-derived timeout (adversarial non-default value)
scenarios.liveTestExpectation: Construct GenericOAuth2 with cfg.AuthOAuthHTTPTimeoutSeconds = 9 (NOT the default 15). Exercise tokenRequest against httptest.NewServer; assert the constructed http.Client.Timeout == 9 * time.Second. Using a non-default value proves the value flows from config rather than being a coincidence with the pre-fix literal.

scenarios.title: Hardcoded HTTP-timeout literal pattern is eradicated from the two affected files
Build and unit test suite pass after the fix
scenarios.liveTestExpectation: `./smackerel.sh build` exit 0; `./smackerel.sh test unit` exit 0 with the 6 new test functions in the output.
